core_behavior: Authenticate and authorize the requesting user before accepting any file data.
Enforce file size limits before or during transfer (fail fast).
Validate the file type using both declared MIME type and deep content inspection (magic bytes).
Scan the file for malware/malicious content before persisting it.
Sanitize the filename and strip or reject dangerous metadata.
Store the file in an isolated storage location not directly accessible via a public URL by default.
Generate a unique, opaque storage key (not derived from the original filename) for the stored file.
Record file upload metadata in a persistent audit log.
Return a stable reference (file ID) to the caller upon success.
Enforce per-user and per-tenant upload quotas.

The system shall provide an endpoint and associated processing pipeline that allows authenticated users to upload files to the platform in a manner that protects the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of the system and its data.
inputs: `Authorization` header: String (Bearer JWT or session token), required — Identifies and authenticates the caller
`file`: Binary (multipart/form-data), required — The file content to be uploaded
`filename`: String, required — Original filename provided by the client
`content_type`: String (MIME), required — Declared MIME type from the client
`target_folder_id`: UUID, optional — Logical folder/collection to associate the file with
Transport: HTTPS only (TLS 1.2 minimum, TLS 1.3 preferred). HTTP requests shall be rejected with `301 Moved Permanently` or `400 Bad Request`.
Encoding: `multipart/form-data` with a single file part. Chunked transfer encoding is supported for large files.
outputs: Success response `201 Created` with JSON body containing: `file_id`, `filename`, `size_bytes`, `content_type`, `checksum_sha256`, `uploaded_at`, `visibility`, `download_url`
`400 Bad Request`: Missing required fields, malformed request, invalid filename
`401 Unauthorized`: Missing or invalid authentication token
`403 Forbidden`: Authenticated but not authorized to upload (role, quota exceeded, target folder access denied)
`409 Conflict`: Duplicate file detected (same SHA-256 hash within the same user scope, if deduplication is enabled)
`413 Content Too Large`: File exceeds maximum allowed size
`415 Unsupported Media Type`: File type not in the allowlist or content inspection mismatch
`422 Unprocessable Entity`: File passed size/type checks but failed malware scan or metadata sanitization
`429 Too Many Requests`: Rate limit exceeded
`500 Internal Server Error`: Storage or processing failure
`503 Service Unavailable`: Malware scanning service or storage backend unavailable
All error responses shall include a JSON body with fields: `error_code`, `message`, `request_id`
failure_modes: `400 Bad Request`: Missing required fields, malformed request, invalid filename
`401 Unauthorized`: Missing or invalid authentication token
`403 Forbidden`: Authenticated but not authorized to upload (role, quota exceeded, target folder access denied)
`409 Conflict`: Duplicate file detected (same SHA-256 hash within the same user scope, if deduplication is enabled)
`413 Content Too Large`: File exceeds maximum allowed size
`415 Unsupported Media Type`: File type not in the allowlist or content inspection mismatch
`422 Unprocessable Entity`: File passed size/type checks but failed malware scan or metadata sanitization
`429 Too Many Requests`: Rate limit exceeded
`500 Internal Server Error`: Storage or processing failure
`503 Service Unavailable`: Malware scanning service or storage backend unavailable
edge_cases: Duplicate file detected (same SHA-256 hash within the same user scope, if deduplication is enabled) — returns `409 Conflict`
Content inspection mismatch between declared MIME type and actual file content — returns `415 Unsupported Media Type`
File passed size/type checks but failed malware scan or metadata sanitization — returns `422 Unprocessable Entity`
Chunked transfer encoding is supported for large files.

Generate a unique, opaque storage key (not derived from the original filename) for the stored file.
Transport: HTTPS only (TLS 1.2 minimum, TLS 1.3 preferred). HTTP requests shall be rejected with `301 Moved Permanently` or `400 Bad Request`.
Enforce per-user and per-tenant upload quotas.
